notes: Dug deeper this round and found actual bugs hiding in the charming exterior. The SVG gradient ID collision is the most embarrassing one — Luna's hair is probably broken in dual-view scenarios and nobody noticed because the Office tab is the default. Also, silent single-source failures and stale relative timestamps are the kind of thing that quietly erodes trust. Code quality drops to 3 this review — the dead CSS and ignored `emoji` field suggest some cleanup debt is accumulating.

suggestions.title: SVG gradient ID 'lunaHairGrad' collides on dual render
suggestions.description: Luna's CharacterAvatar SVG hardcodes id="lunaHairGrad" for her hair gradient. SVG IDs must be unique per HTML document — if Luna appears in both the Office tab and a Dashboard worker card simultaneously, the second instance references the first's gradient definition (wrong orientation/colors) or gets nothing. The fix is to use a unique ID per render, e.g. suffix with a React key or use a linearGradient with a data URI paint server. This is a real rendering bug, not a hypothetical.

suggestions.title: Single-source fetch failure is completely silent
suggestions.description: In syncFromGitHub(), the fetchError banner only appears when BOTH requests (workers + activity) fail. If only workers.json fails, isLive flips to false and worker data goes stale, but no user-facing error is shown — users stare at outdated cards with zero indication anything is wrong. The fix: set fetchError (or a separate workersError) whenever workersResult.status === 'rejected', regardless of activity result.

suggestions.title: ProgressBar has zero ARIA semantics
suggestions.description: The ProgressBar component renders a styled div with no role="progressbar", no aria-valuenow, no aria-valuemin, and no aria-valuemax. Screen readers cannot perceive or announce task progress at all. This is a straightforward fix: add role="progressbar" aria-valuenow={progress} aria-valuemin={0} aria-valuemax={100} to the progress-bar-bg div.

suggestions.title: ARIA tab pattern is incomplete — missing IDs and aria-labelledby
suggestions.description: The tab buttons have role="tab" and aria-selected, which is good. But they have no id attributes, and the role="tabpanel" divs have no aria-labelledby to link back to their controlling tab. The ARIA tabs pattern requires: each tab button gets an id (e.g. tab-office), each panel gets aria-labelledby pointing to that id, and each panel gets its own id for aria-controls on the button. As-is, assistive technology cannot announce which panel belongs to which tab.

suggestions.title: Activity log relative timestamps go stale and never refresh
suggestions.description: getRelativeTime() is called during render to compute '5m ago', '2h ago', etc. After the initial load, these strings are frozen in time — there is no interval or effect to re-render the activity entries on a tick. Leave the page open for 30 minutes and everything still shows its original relative time. Fix: either add a useEffect with a 60-second setInterval that forces a re-render of the log, or use a live clock stored in state.

suggestions.title: 'Creative Director' missing from ROLE_EMOJIS and ROLE_COLORS
suggestions.description: Luna's role is 'Creative Director' but this string appears in neither ROLE_EMOJIS (App.jsx) nor ROLE_COLORS (Office.jsx). In the Dashboard, Luna's worker card displays the generic robot emoji 🤖 fallback. In the Office generic fallback SVG, she'd render with grey (#6b7280) body color. Luna has a custom avatar so she's fine in the Office, but any future worker added with this role title in the Dashboard will look like an unnamed bot. Add 'Creative Director': '🎭' to ROLE_EMOJIS and a suitable color to ROLE_COLORS.

suggestions.title: Roster 'emoji' field from workers.json is fetched but never consumed
suggestions.description: The roster entries in workers.json each carry an 'emoji' property (e.g. Luna: '🌙', Carlos: '⚙️'). However, getRoleEmoji() in App.jsx only looks up ROLE_EMOJIS[role] and never reads worker.emoji. The WorkerCard passes worker to getRoleEmoji but only uses the role string. This means the data model supports per-character emoji overrides but the UI ignores them — dead data that adds confusion. Either use worker.emoji as a priority override in getRoleEmoji, or remove the field from the data to reduce maintenance confusion.

suggestions.title: Office character tooltips are CSS-only and keyboard/SR inaccessible
suggestions.description: Task tooltips on office characters use CSS ::after pseudo-elements triggered by :hover. They are invisible to keyboard navigation and screen readers entirely. Characters with pointer-events re-enabled (.char--working, .char--idle, .char--ghost) can be hovered by mouse, but there is no focus state, no aria-label, and no title attribute. Given the office view has role="img" and aria-label, individual characters are not exposed to AT at all. Consider adding focusable wrappers with aria-label="{name}: {task}" to working/idle characters.

suggestions.title: Dead CSS: .stat-card.done styles with no matching DOM element
suggestions.description: App.css contains .stat-card.done .stat-value { color: var(--blue); } but the StatsBar component only renders cards with classes total, active, idle, and sync-status — 'done' is never used. This suggests a stat for completed workers was removed from the UI but the CSS was left behind. It's harmless but signals incomplete cleanup and will confuse anyone maintaining the stylesheet.

suggestions.title: color-mix() in .hq-badge has no fallback for older browsers
suggestions.description: The .hq-badge class uses color-mix(in srgb, var(--accent) 12%, transparent) for background and border colors. color-mix() is only supported from Chrome 111, Firefox 113, and Safari 16.2 — any browser before that renders the badge with no background or border at all. A simple rgba fallback before the color-mix line would provide basic styling: background: rgba(124, 106, 247, 0.12); background: color-mix(...).
